findings.description: Critical: the shipped app never renders the sync-point gate banner. `App` returns only `<Board issues={issues} />`, so the required pinned banner, 5s polling, clear/blocked state, and kick entry point are absent from the actual UI despite `SyncPointBanner` existing in isolation.

Critical: vendor swimlanes are not integrated into cards. `Card` renders only title/change/assignee/timestamp and never imports or receives `VendorSwimlanes` data; `useCoordinator` also drops audit events instead of storing latest activity, so in-flight cards cannot show per-vendor lanes or live audit summaries.

Critical: SSE live transition/audit events are effectively filtered out. `sse_event_generator` drops any event without `evt.change_id`, but the existing `coordinator_notify` payloads and the new audit trigger do not populate `change_id`, so database NOTIFY events from real work_queue/audit_log changes will never reach subscribed clients.

Critical: multi-change subscriptions fetch the intersection of change labels, not the union. Both `fetchIssues()` and `_build_snapshot()` pass all `change:<id>` labels in one `POST /issues/list`/`IssueService.list_issues` call, but `IssueService` documents and implements label filtering as 'must contain ALL specified', so subscribing to `change_ids=a,b` hides normal issues that belong to only one change.

Critical: the banner kick action cannot clear a real registry entry. The UI hard-codes `changeId` to `"unknown"` when opening the consent prompt, while the backend kick endpoint requires the real change_id to run `worktree.py teardown <change_id> --agent-id ...`; the confirm handler also treats any HTTP response as success without checking `res.ok` or `kicked`.

Critical: the requested additive audit filters were implemented on `/audit/v2`, while the existing `GET /audit` route still accepts only `agent_id`, `operation`, and `limit`. The spec and polling-fallback contract call for `GET /audit?since=...&change_id=...`, so clients following the contract will not get scoped audit activity.

Critical: saved-view, drag-to-Ready, and force-release-lock UI surfaces are marked complete in tasks but are not present in the app. `saveView()` and the reversibility table exist as library functions, but there is no `SavedViewsDrawer`, no drag handler calling `PATCH /issues/{id}/labels`, and no force-release flow wired into the rendered UI.

Critical: the token-in-URL mitigation is incomplete. The contracts require access logs to redact `token=`, but the `/events/work` handler accepts the JWT as a query parameter and there is no redaction middleware/logger configuration in this change, so enabling coordinator access logs can leak short-lived SSE JWTs.

Nit: the stale duplicate `skills/tests/agent-coordinator/test_kanban_viz_endpoints.py` from OC-1 still exists with old sys.path manipulation. It is misleading dead code beside the real `agent-coordinator/tests/test_kanban_viz_endpoints.py` and should be removed to avoid future collection/import confusion.
